one_time_description: Displacement - Community Recovery expenses, a non-recurring item with no budget amount
economic_notes: Nation owns 99.99% interest in Fort A La Corne Ventures LP (GBE, recorded under modified equity). JSCN Medical Clinic LP is a related entity with BMO loan guarantees. Multiple active land claims and Treaty Land Entitlement negotiations ongoing. Significant capital projects including school assessment, flood recovery roads, subdivision expansion, lagoon, and solid waste transfer station.
notes: Fiscal year is March 31, 2023 (not 2024). Nation is a consolidated entity of three First Nation sectors: James Smith Cree Nation, Chakastaypasin Band of the Cree Nation, and Peter Chapman Band. Consolidated entities include James Smith Cree Nation Operations and CMHC Housing Fund. Fort A La Corne Ventures LP is accounted for under modified equity method as a GBE with a cumulative loss of $3,075,386. Significant off-balance-sheet loan guarantees totalling over $17.5M (Government of Canada negotiation loans $4.99M, RBC loans $8.79M, BMO loans to JSCN Medical Clinic LP $3.81M). BMO loans were refinanced subsequent to year end. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $21,393,526 + Health Canada $5,196,321 + CMHC grants $530,677 + PAGC $370,580 = $27,490,124 (CMHC rental income and PAGC portion treated as transfers). No schedule of remuneration provided in the extracted text. Displacement-Community Recovery of $3,382,387 flagged as one-time non-recurring item.
